dns_explorer.domain.ports.idns_resolver module¶

Module

idns_resolver.py

Info

Defines abstract interface for DNS resolution operations.

class dns_explorer.domain.ports.idns_resolver.IDNSResolver[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Abstract interface for DNS resolution.

It defines:

attributes:

None.

methods:
resolve - Resolves a domain to an IP address.
reverse_resolve - Performs reverse DNS lookup for an IP address.
resolve_record - Queries specific DNS records for a domain.
is_initialized - Checks if the resolver is initialized.
__str__ - Returns the DNSResolver as string representation.
_abc_impl = <_abc._abc_data object>¶
abstract is_initialized() → bool[source]¶

Checks if the resolver is initialized.

Returns:

True if initialized, False otherwise.

Return type:

<bool>

Exceptions:

None.

abstract resolve(domain: str) → str | None[source]¶

Resolves a domain name to an IP address.

Parameters:

domain (<str>) – The domain name to resolve.

Returns:

The resolved IP address or None if resolution fails.

Return type:

<str | None>

Exceptions:

None.

abstract resolve_record(domain: str, record_type: str) → list[str][source]¶

Queries specific DNS records for a domain.

Parameters:

  • domain (<str>) – The domain name.

  • record_type (<str>) – The DNS record type (A, MX, etc.).

Returns:

List of record values.

Return type:

<list[str]>

Exceptions:

None.

abstract reverse_resolve(ip: str) → list[str] | None[source]¶

Performs reverse DNS lookup on an IP address.

Parameters:

ip (<str>) – The IP address.

Returns:

The list of resolved hostnames or None if lookup fails.

Return type:

<list[str] | None>

Exceptions:

None.
